page: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a compute-oriented virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware, though the instance is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory performance represents a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score falls in the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks is weak, landing in the bottom 25%. This low memory throughput impacts memory-intensive workloads, as evidenced by weak database operations and software compilation benchmarks. However, text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression show strong performance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D4als_v6 offers a resource-dense compute profile for workloads that require high clock speeds but have minimal memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution without the performance degradation associated with shared-core instances. This server is best suited for compute-bound, single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic utility workloads. It is less suitable for large-scale database operations, memory-intensive applications, or complex multi-threaded compilations due to its constrained memory capacity and lower memory bandwidth.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-oriented virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of system memory, and no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, but weak memory bandwidth and a poor Passmark memory score. This low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core makes it a cost-efficient choice for compute-bound, single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving. However, it is less suited for memory-intensive databases or software compilation due to memory throughput limitations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a compute-oriented virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance but has limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an AMD-powered virtual machine with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for single-core compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated compute instance featuring strong single-core AMD performance and a streamlined memory profile for efficient, CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Configured with 8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per vCPU).
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing.
Lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators.
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving.
Constrained by low memory bandwidth and limited memory capacity.
